task_completion.visual_evidence: WPS Spreadsheet application is open, displaying the file 'retail_catalog.xlsx' with 'Raw_Products' and 'Margin_Rules' sheet tabs visible at the bottom.

task_completion.description: Populate SKU (Column F) and Margin_Pct (Column G) in the 'Raw_Products' sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Raw_Products' sheet, Column F contains alphanumeric strings (3 uppercase letters followed by numbers), and Column G contains decimal or percentage values.

task_completion.description: Calculate Retail_Price (Column H) and Stock_Value (Column I) in the 'Raw_Products' sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Column H contains numerical values rounded to 2 decimal places, and Column I contains numerical values representing the product of Unit_Cost and Stock_Level.

task_completion.description: Populate Status (Column J) in the 'Raw_Products' sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Column J contains the text 'High Value Low Stock' or 'Standard' for the rows.
A sheet tab named 'Catalog_Summary' is visible at the bottom of the WPS Spreadsheet window.

task_completion.description: Calculate and display the required metrics in the 'Catalog_Summary' sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Catalog_Summary' sheet is active and displays labels for 'Total Stock Value', 'Average Retail Price', and 'High Value Alerts' alongside their corresponding calculated numerical values.

integrity.description: The agent used the WPS Spreadsheet GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the WPS Spreadsheet interface (menus, formula bar, cells) rather than terminal commands or Python scripts modifying the file.

integrity.description: The agent used spreadsheet formulas rather than manually typing calculated answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: When a calculated cell is selected, the formula bar shows an Excel formula (e.g., IF, SUM, AVERAGE, VLOOKUP, arithmetic operations) instead of a hardcoded number or string.

integrity.description: The agent did not copy-paste the final expected summary values directly from an external script or text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The summary values are derived from the data in 'Raw_Products' (ideally visible via formulas in the formula bar) rather than being typed in directly without calculation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is /home/ga/Documents/retail_catalog.xlsx. The final workbook must contain exactly three sheets: 'Raw_Products', 'Margin_Rules', and 'Catalog_Summary'. The VLM should verify that the 'Catalog_Summary' sheet contains the three specific metrics: Total Stock Value, Average Retail Price, and High Value Alerts.
